interaction_phrase: launched in 2019 by Amazon Web Services (AWS)
occurrence_sentence: [Amazon Braket](https://aws.amazon.com/), launched in 2019 by Amazon Web Services (AWS), is a fully managed quantum computing service that provides researchers, developers, and enterprises with unified, on-demand access to a broad array of quantum hardware technologies and simulation tools.

Braket supports a diverse set of hardware from leading providers, including superconducting systems from Rigetti Computing, Oxford Quantum Circuits, neutral atom from QuEra, ion-trap systems from IonQ, quantum annealers from D-Wave, and photonic devices from Xanadu.

occurrence_sentence: Founded in 2013 as a collaboration between Google, NASA, and the Universities Space Research Association, the Quantum Artificial Intelligence Lab quickly became a hub for innovation.

Google's long-term goal is to develop large-scale, error-corrected systems capable of solving real-world problems in fields like logistics, artificial intelligence, quantum chemistry, and foundational computer science.

Google's quantum hardware is [based on the Sycamore architecture](https://qmunity.thequantuminsider.com/2024/04/29/quantum-supremacy-with-sycamore/), with recent progress in quantum error correction enabling logical qubits to outperform physical ones.

As of 2024, Google's 101-qubit device achieved a[ 0.143% error-per-cycle rate](https://thequantuminsider.com/2024/08/27/breaking-the-surface-google-demonstrates-error-correction-below-surface-code-threshold/), improving logical qubit lifetimes by a factor of 2.4.

The company also introduced[ tools like AlphaQubit](https://thequantuminsider.com/2024/11/20/ai-power-for-quantum-errors-google-develops-alphaqubit-to-identify-correct-quantum-errors/), an AI-powered decoder that accelerates error correction, and [LUCI](https://thequantuminsider.com/2024/10/23/luci-in-the-surface-codes-with-drop-outs-google-quantum-ai-researchers-report-framework-could-help-reduce-errors/), a dynamic framework for fault-tolerant quantum circuit adaptation that reduces logical error rates by a factor of 36.
On the software side, Google has created a rich ecosystem for quantum algorithm development.
Google Quantum AI continues to explore the fusion of quantum computing with artificial intelligence, enabling hybrid classical-quantum models to prototype machine learning solutions.
The team is also advancing hardware scalability through investments in cryogenic infrastructure and modular hybrid architectures.
